Dear Ms. McGee:
This is to confirm my understanding of our telephone
conversation today. As I stated the facts, Senator Boatwright
has an interest in real property in Sacramento, a trprincipalrl
residence in which he has not resided for three years. The home
is jointly 
owned by he and his vrife. As such, this home has not
been reported in his annual statements of economic interest. 
The
couple are separated, and a divorce proceeding will be final
shortly. 
His wife continues to reside in the home. He receives
no income from his joint ownership of the home.
On December 28, l-990, Senator Boatwright purchased a new
home, and now maintains this as his principal or primary
residence.
My question was whether or not he is required to list either
or both of the homes on his statement of economic interest for
the year ending December 31, 1990. After looking into the
matter, you responded to ne that he would not have to report
either residence in his statement of economic interest.
Cou1d you please confirm this in writing at your earliest
opportunity, keeping in mind the March 7-, 1991 due date for the
report?
Thank you again for your prornpt advice on this matter.
Sincerely, ----.
Sarr\S-e;il^r'

sections.question: My question was whether or not he is required to list either or both of the homes on his statement of economic interest for the year ending December 31, 1990.
sections.question_synthetic: Must Senator Boatwright report either or both of his residences (a principal residence he has not occupied for three years and a newly purchased home as of December 28, 1990) on his statement of economic interest for the year ending December 31, 1990?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/follow-up letter, not an FPPC advice letter response. The letter references prior telephone advice from FPPC staff (Colleen McGee) stating properties would not require reporting, but requests written confirmation. No formal CONCLUSION section exists as this is the incoming request, not the FPPC's response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: My question was whether or not he is required to list either or both of the homes on his statement of economic interest for the year ending December 31, 1990.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from Senator Boatwright's staff to FPPC Enforcement seeking written confirmation of prior telephone advice regarding whether two residential properties must be reported on the Senator's statement of economic interest for 1990.
